title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: **Findings** - `Critical`: CI cannot validate global prompt content, only repo adapter text. Both gates explicitly set `ALLOW_MISSING_GLOBAL_PROMPTS=1`, so missing `~/.codex/prompts/*` becomes a pass-by-skip path. See [fugue-orchestrator gate](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml:142), [feed-runner gate](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-feed-runner/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml:92), [kernel check skip branch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:123), [vote check skip branch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh:123). - `High`: There is no repo-visible version pin for the intended global prompt contract. CI can pass while operators are on different home prompt revisions. Current global prompt contracts are only in home files: [kernel prompt](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:1), [vote prompt](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/vote.md:1). - `High`: Smoke checks prove behavior text, but not contract identity/version. They confirm marker output from `codex exec`, not “which prompt revision” was intended. See [kernel smoke runner](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:79), [vote smoke runner](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh:79). - `Medium`: Hardcoded launcher path is machine-specific and weakens portability of the verification layer. See [kernel check launcher path](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:13), [vote check launcher path](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh:13). - `Policy note`: Treating “missing global prompts” as normal in CI is effectively a single-lane fallback on adapter-only checks; that weakens the parallel/independent verification objective. **Recommended implementation shape (one option)** - Add one checked-in lock file per repo, e.g. `config/codex-global-prompt-lock.json`, containing: - `contract_version` (human-readable pin) - `source` (`~/.codex/prompts`) - `files.kernel/k/vote/v.sha256` - `updated_at` - Add one local sync script (run by humans locally) that reads `~/.codex/prompts/{kernel,k,vote,v}.md`, computes hashes, and updates the lock. - In CI, validate only the lock schema + required keys + that docs/check scripts reference the lock version. This gives CI a repo-visible, deterministic statement of “which global prompt contract is intended” without reintroducing prompt copies.
